title: pavonia macrostyla
intro: Pavonia macrostyla is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hollyhock or mallows family, and is native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. The health benefits of Pavonia macrostyla include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, and it has also been used as a treatment for wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
